# GetQibla

> GetQibla is a free, open-source, mobile-first Qibla direction finder. It calculates the initial great-circle bearing to the Kaaba and uses a supported phone compass for a live arrow. Location and sensor readings remain in the browser.

## Compass

- English: /
- Urdu: /ur/
- Indonesian: /id/
- Arabic: /ar/
- Turkish: /tr/

## Guides

- Find Qibla on a phone: /how-to-find-qibla-direction/
- Understand the Qibla angle: /qibla-angle/
- Fix a Qibla compass: /qibla-compass-not-working/
- Qibla on iPhone: /qibla-direction-iphone/
- Qibla on Android: /qibla-direction-android/
- Find Qibla without a phone compass: /find-qibla-without-compass/
- Calculation method and limits: /how-qibla-is-calculated/
- Privacy: /privacy/

## Verified city bearings

- New York: /qibla-direction/new-york/
- Boston: /qibla-direction/boston/
- London: /qibla-direction/london/
- Karachi: /qibla-direction/karachi/
- Toronto: /qibla-direction/toronto/
- Dubai: /qibla-direction/dubai/
- Sydney: /qibla-direction/sydney/
- Machine-readable dataset: /data/qibla-bearings.json

## Method

- Kaaba coordinates: 21.4224779° N, 39.8251832° E.
- Bearings are initial great-circle bearings measured clockwise from true north.
- The live arrow compares the calculated bearing with browser-provided device orientation.
- Phone readings can be affected by sensor support, calibration, tilt, magnetic declination, metal and magnets.
- The project does not claim perfect compass accuracy.

## Source

- Repository: https://github.com/okHadi/getqibla
- License: MIT
